questions.question: A motor lifts a weight, doing $240\text{ J}$ of useful work. The motor consumes $300\text{ J}$ of electrical energy. What is the efficiency of the motor?
A machine has an efficiency of $75\%$ and receives $500\text{ J}$ of input energy. How much useful work does it do?
A pulley system lifts a load, doing $180\text{ J}$ of useful work with an efficiency of $60\%$. How much work was input?
An engine with $35\%$ efficiency does $700\text{ J}$ of useful work. How much energy is wasted?
questions.explanation: Work input $= 700 / 0.35 = 2,000\text{ J}$. Wasted energy $= 2,000 - 700 = 1,300\text{ J}$. Alternatively, $65\%$ of $2,000\text{ J} = 1,300\text{ J}$.

questions.question: A water pump outputs $450\text{ W}$ of useful power while drawing $600\text{ W}$ of electrical power. What is its efficiency?
A motor rated at $200\text{ W}$ has an efficiency of $65\%$. What is the useful power output?
A crane does $540\text{ W}$ of useful lifting power at $90\%$ efficiency. What power does it draw?
Machine P converts $200\text{ J}$ of input into $150\text{ J}$ of useful output. Machine Q converts $300\text{ J}$ of input into $210\text{ J}$ of useful output. Which machine is more efficient?
questions.explanation: Machine P: $150/200 \times 100\% = 75\%$. Machine Q: $210/300 \times 100\% = 70\%$. Machine P is more efficient.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains why the efficiency of a real machine is always less than $100\%$?
options: Some input energy is always converted to heat due to friction
Efficiency cannot be calculated for moving parts
The input energy is always less than the useful output
questions.explanation: In real machines, some energy is always lost as heat from friction, sound, or other resistive forces. Efficiency is always less than $100\%$.

questions.question: A machine receives $800\text{ J}$ of input and operates at $62.5\%$ efficiency. How much energy is wasted?
A machine does $450\text{ J}$ of useful work and wastes $150\text{ J}$ as heat and sound. What is its efficiency?
An incandescent light bulb draws $100\text{ W}$ of electrical power but only converts $8\text{ W}$ into visible light. The rest is wasted as heat. What is the efficiency of the bulb?
questions.explanation: Efficiency $= \frac{8}{100} \times 100\% = 8\%$. Most energy in incandescent bulbs is lost as heat.

scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review solving for work input when given output and efficiency.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on the relationship: Efficiency = (Useful output / Total input) × 100%.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: efficiency is always less than 100%, and energy is conserved (input = useful output + wasted).
